learningObjectives.title: Understand transportation planning
learningObjectives.description: Explain how transportation demand is transformed into executable freight plans.

learningObjectives.title: Understand planning inputs and outputs
learningObjectives.description: Identify key planning inputs and resulting freight orders, assignments and schedules.

learningObjectives.title: Understand optimization impact
learningObjectives.description: Describe how SAP TM planning improves utilization, cost and service outcomes.

sections.content: Transportation Planning is the core functionality of SAP Transportation Management. It transforms transportation demand into executable transportation plans by consolidating Freight Units, selecting transportation lanes, assigning carriers and resources, and creating Freight Orders while minimizing cost and maximizing resource utilization.

sections.title: Key Concept
sections.content: Transportation Planning converts transportation demand (Freight Units) into executable transportation documents (Freight Orders).

A company receives 20 customer deliveries destined for nearby locations. SAP TM consolidates them into four Freight Orders based on truck capacity, delivery dates and transportation zones, reducing freight cost while maintaining on-time delivery.

sections.title: Manual Planning vs SAP TM Planning
Best Practice
sections.content: Maintain accurate master data and planning profiles before enabling automatic transportation planning. The optimizer is only as good as the master data it uses.

sections.title: Real Project Example
sections.content: A retail company consolidated 350 daily deliveries into 95 optimized Freight Orders using SAP TM. Vehicle utilization increased by 28%, and transportation costs decreased by 17%.

sections.title: Interview Tip
sections.content: The first step in Transportation Planning is planning Freight Units, not Sales Orders or Freight Orders. Freight Orders are the result of the planning process.

sections.title: Common Mistake
sections.content: Do not assume Transportation Planning begins with Freight Orders. Freight Orders are created only after transportation demand has been planned and optimized.

items: Transportation Planning converts Freight Units into Freight Orders.
Planning includes optimization, carrier selection and resource assignment.
SAP TM improves vehicle utilization and reduces freight cost.
Freight Orders are the primary output of transportation planning.
